title: AI Recommendations
description: How the offline rule engine and Claude AI work together to give you plant care advice.

sections.body: Plantgotchi uses a two-tier system to keep you informed about your plant's needs — an instant offline rule engine and a periodic AI advisor.

sections.heading: Tier 1: Offline Rule Engine
sections.body:
Every time a new sensor reading arrives, the rule engine evaluates it against your plant's thresholds. This happens instantly on your device — no internet needed.

The rules that are checked on every reading:

ConditionSeverityExample Alert
Moisture below minimumWarning (Critical if <20%)"Needs water! Soil moisture at 18%"
Moisture above maximumWarning"May be overwatered — moisture at 92%"
Temperature below minimumWarning (Critical if >5°C below)"Too cold! 8°C (minimum: 15°C)"
Temperature above maximumWarning (Critical if >5°C above)"Too hot! 37°C (maximum: 30°C)"
Battery below 15%Warning (Critical if <5%)"Sensor battery low — charge soon"
Light too low for preferenceInfo"Prefers bright light but only getting 500 lux"

If all readings are within range, no recommendations are generated — your plant is happy!

sections.heading: Tier 2: Claude AI Advisor
sections.body:
Every 6 hours (when online), Plantgotchi sends your plant's recent data to Claude AI for a personalized recommendation. The AI receives:

  • Your plant's name, species, and care preferences
  • The last 7 days of sensor readings (moisture, temperature trends)
  • Your last 10 care actions (when you watered, fertilized, etc.)

Claude analyzes the trends — not just the current reading — and provides a single, actionable sentence. For example: "Your monstera's soil has been consistently dry; consider increasing watering frequency to twice a week."

sections.heading: Recommendation Severity
sections.body:
AI recommendations are automatically tagged with a severity level:

  • Urgent — the AI says "immediately" or "urgent" (act now)
  • Warning — mentions "should," "consider," or "watch" (act soon)
  • Info — general tips and observations (good to know)

sections.heading: Viewing Recommendations
sections.body: All recommendations (from both the rule engine and AI) appear in your plant's detail view under the Recommendations section. Each shows its source ("rules" or "claude"), severity, and timestamp. You can mark recommendations as "acted on" to clear them.
